name: orto

metadata.name: orto
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: A package to make life easier when performing Orca calculations.

documentation:
`orto` is a package to make life easier when performing Orca calculations.



Please see the `orto` documentation for more details.

functions.name: orto.utils.platform_check
documentation:
Decorator to check platform for color terminal output.

Windows Anaconda prompt will not support colors by default, so
colors are disabled for all windows machines, unless the
orto_termcolor envvar is defined

functions.name: orto.utils.cprint
documentation:
Prints colorised output to screen

Parameters
----------
string: str
    String to print
color: str {red, green, yellow, blue, magenta, cyan, white, black_yellowbg, white_bluebg}
    String name of color

Returns
-------
None

functions.name: orto.utils.get_opt_coords
documentation:
Extracts coordinates from orca optimisation cycle

Parameters
----------
file_name: str
    Name of file to check

Returns
-------
list[str]
    Labels
list[float]
    Coordinates (3,n_atoms)
bool
    True if stationary point found

functions.name: orto.utils.get_input_section
documentation: Extracts Input section from orca output file

functions.name: orto.utils.check_envvar
documentation:
Checks specified environment variable has been defined, exits program if
variable is not defined

Parameters

None

functions.name: orto.utils.get_envvar
documentation:
Gets specified environment variable
If undefined then returns empty string

functions.name: orto.utils.orbname_to_mathmode
documentation: Converts Orca AI-LFT orbital names to mathmode strings

functions.name: orto.plotter.gaussian
documentation:
Gaussian g(x) with given peak position (b), fwhm, and area

g(x) = area/(c*sqrt(2pi)) * exp(-(x-b)**2/(2c**2))

c = fwhm/(2*np.sqrt(2*np.log(2)))

Parameters
----------
x : array_like
    Continuous variable
fwhm: float
    Full Width at Half-Maximum
b : float
    Peak position
area : float
    Area of Gaussian function

Return
------
list[float]
    g(x) at each value of x

functions.name: orto.plotter.lorentzian
documentation:
Lotenztian L(x) with given peak position (b), fwhm, and area

L(x) = (0.5*area*fwhm/pi) * 1/((x-x0)**2 + (0.5*fwhm)**2)

Parameters
----------
x : array_like
    Continuous variable
fwhm: float
    Full Width at Half-Maximum
x0 : float
    Peak position
area : float
    Area of Lorentzian function

Return
------
list[float]
    L(x) at each value of x

functions.name: orto.plotter.plot_abs
documentation:
Plots absorption spectrum with oscillator strengths specifying intensity

Parameters
----------
wavenumbers: array_like
    Wavenumber of eac transition [cm^-1]
foscs: array_like
    Oscillator strength of each transition
lineshape: str {'gaussian', 'lorentzian'}
    Lineshape function to use for each transition/signal
linewidth: float
    Linewidth used in lineshape [cm^-1]
x_lim: list[float], default [0., 2000]
    Minimum and maximum x-values to plot [cm^-1 or nm]
y_lim: list[float | str], default 'auto'
    Minimum and maximum y-values to plot [cm^-1 mol^-1 L]
x_unit: str {'wavenumber', 'wavelength'}
    X-unit to use, data will be converted internally.

    Assumes cm^-1 for wavenumber and nm for wavelength.
abs_type: str {'napierian', 'logarithmic'}
    Absorbance (and epsilon) type to use. Orca_mapspc uses napierian
show_osc: bool, default True
    If True, show oscillator strength stemplots
save: bool, default False
    If True, plot is saved to save_name
save_name: str
    If save is True, plot is saved to this location/filename
show: bool, default False
    If True, plot is shown on screen
window_title: str, default 'UV-Visible Absorption Spectrum'
    Title of figure window, not of plot
Returns
-------
plt.Figure
    Matplotlib Figure object
list[plt.Axes]
    Matplotlib Axis object for main plot followed by

    Matplotlib Axis object for twinx oscillator strength axis

functions.name: orto.plotter.plot_chit
documentation:
Plots susceptibility*T data as a function of temperature

Parameters
----------
chit: array_like
    Susceptibility * Temperature
temps: array_like
    Temperatures in Kelvin
y_unit: str, default 'cm^3\ K\ mol^{-1}'
    Mathmode y-unit which matches input chit data
save: bool, default False
    If True, plot is saved to save_name
save_name: str, default 'chit_vs_t.png'
    If save is True, plot is saved to this location/filename
show: bool, default False
    If True, plot is shown on screen
window_title: str, default 'Calculated Susceptibility'
    Title of figure window, not of plot
Returns
-------
plt.Figure
    Matplotlib Figure object
plt.Axes
    Matplotlib Axis object for plot

functions.name: orto.plotter.plot_ir
documentation: Plots Infrared Spectrum

functions.name: orto.plotter.plot_raman
documentation: Plots Raman Spectrum

functions.name: orto.plotter.plot_cd
documentation: Plots circular dichroism data

functions.name: orto.plotter.plot_ailft_orb_energies
documentation:
Parameters
----------
energies: array_like
    Energies which are in same unit as y_unit
labels: array_like | None, optional
    If provided, labels are added next to energy levels.
groups: array_like | None, optional
    If provided, groups orbitals together by offsetting x coordinate
y_unit: str, default 'cm^{-1}'
    Mathmode y-unit which matches input chit data
save: bool, default False
    If True, plot is saved to save_name
save_name: str, default 'ai_lft_energies.png'
    If save is True, plot is saved to this location/filename
show: bool, default False
    If True, plot is shown on screen
window_title: str, default 'AI-LFT Orbital Energies'
    Title of figure window, not of plot

Returns
-------
plt.Figure
    Matplotlib Figure object
plt.Axes
    Matplotlib Axis object for plot

functions.name: orto.extractor.get_coords
documentation:
Extracts cartesian coordinates and atom labels from Orca output file

Parameters
----------
file_name: str | pathlib.Path
    Orca output file to parse
coord_type: str, {'init', 'opt'}
    Specifies which set of coordinates to extract

    Options are:

    "init" = Initial coordinates

    "opt" = Final optimised coordinates
index_style: str {'per_element', 'sequential', 'sequential_orca', 'none'}
    Specifies what type of atom label indexing used for final atom labels

    Options are:

    'per_element' = Index by element e.g. Dy1, Dy2, N1, N2, etc.

    'sequential' = Index the atoms 1->N regardless of element

    'sequential_orca' = Index the atoms 0->N-1 regardless of element

    'none' = No label indexing

Returns
-------
list
    Atomic labels
ndarray of floats
    (n_atoms,3) array containing xyz coordinates of each atom

classes.name: orto.extractor.SusceptibilityExtractor

methods.name: orto.extractor.SusceptibilityExtractor.data
documentation:
Processed susceptibility data, one dataframe per extracted block.

For each entry, column titles are 

 - Static Field (Gauss)

 - Temperature (K)

 - M/B: chi*T (cm3*K/mol)

 - chi*T (cm3*K/mol)

documentation:
Converts single block into array of susceptibility as a function of
temperature

Parameters
----------

Returns
-------

    Susceptibility dataframe with columns described in self.data

classes.name: orto.extractor.ExchangeCouplingExtractor

methods.name: orto.extractor.ExchangeCouplingExtractor.data
documentation:
Processed Exchange coupling analysis, one dict per block
For each list entry, keys are 

 - S(High-Spin)

 - <S**2>(High-Spin)

 - <S**2>(BrokenSym)

 - E(High-Spin) (Eh)

 - E(BrokenSym) (Eh)

 - E(High-Spin)-E(BrokenSym) (cm^-1)
 - J(1) (cm^-1)
 - J(2) (cm^-1)
 - J(3) (cm^-1)

and all values are numpy arrays of shape (n_orbs, n_orbs)

classes.name: orto.extractor.FrequencyExtractor

methods.name: orto.extractor.FrequencyExtractor.data
documentation:
Processed Exchange coupling analysis, one dict per block
For each dict, keys are 

 - frequency (cm^-1)

 - displacements - shape: (n_atoms, 3 * n_atoms, 3)

 - epsilon (L mol^-1 cm^-1)

 - tx

 - ty

 - tz

and all values are numpy arrays.

Dimensions are n_atoms*3, 1 for all arrays other than displacements.

methods.name: orto.extractor.FrequencyExtractor._process_block

    Keys described in self.data

Extracts Vibrational mode energies, eigenvectors, and intensity information

classes.name: orto.extractor.LoewdinCompositionExtractor

methods.name: orto.extractor.LoewdinCompositionExtractor.data
documentation:
Processed Loewdin Natural orbital data
Each data entry is a tuple containing:

First entry - a list of dicts, one dict per Natural orbital

    Keys:

        ATOM_NUMBER ATOM_SYMBOL ORBITAL_NAME

    Values

        % Contribution to orbital

Second entry - a list of occupation numbers, one float per orbital

methods.name: orto.extractor.LoewdinCompositionExtractor._process_block
documentation:
Converts single block into data entries described in self.data

       Parameters
       ----------
       block: str
           String block extracted from file

       Returns
       -------
       tuple[list[dict[str, float]], list[float]]
           First entry - a list of dicts, one dict per Natural orbital

               Keys:

                   ATOM_NUMBER ATOM_SYMBOL ORBITAL_NAME

               Values

                   % Contribution to orbital

           Second entry - a list of occupation numbers, one float per orbital
# noqa

classes.name: orto.job.OrcaJob

methods.name: orto.job.OrcaJob.write_script
documentation:
Writes submission script to file

Parameters
----------
verbose: bool, default True
    If True, jobscript location is written to screen

Class to generate submission scripts for ORCA calculation



Submission script format is 


...............

SCHEDULER BLOCK

...............

LOAD_BLOCK

...............

PRE-ORCA_BLOCK

...............

$(which orca) input_file > output_file

...............

POST-ORCA_BLOCK

...............


Attributes
----------
input_file: pathlib.Path
    Orca input file for which a submission script is created
output_file: pathlib.Path
    Orca output file name. Default is same as input with extension 

    replaced by .out
job_file: pathlib.Path
    Submission script name. Default is same as input with extension 

    replaced by scheduler job file extension e.g. '.slm'
job_name: str
    Job name. Default is same as input without extension
scheduler_name: str {'slurm'}
    Scheduler system name
pre_orca: str
    Commands for pre-orca block
post_orca: str
    Commands for post-orca block
